name: Bittersweet Chocolate Coated Truffles
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Peanut Butter, Almond Butter, or Walnut Pieces
recipeInstructions:
For Truffles:
1. Melt the chocolate in a double boiler, stirring constantly. Alternatively, melt it in the microwave for 2 to 3 minutes on medium power.
2. Remove the melted chocolate from heat and stir in the butter.
3. Stir in the heavy whipping cream and liqueur (up to 2 tablespoons).
4. If desired, combine with chopped nuts or candied fruit.
5. Chill for 10-15 minutes, stirring frequently until thick enough to hold a shape.
6. Drop by heaping teaspoon or #70 scoop onto foil-lined baking pan.
7. Shape into balls if desired and cover with plastic wrap.
8. Freeze for 20-30 minutes to set the truffles before dipping.
For Coating:
1. Melt the chocolate as directed in the basic truffle recipe.
2. Remove from heat and stir in the oil.
3. Cool the chocolate to 85-90°F (29-32°C) for dipping.
4. Dip the chilled, firm truffles into the melted chocolate, holding with a fork or spooning several coats of chocolate.
5. Place each dipped truffle onto a foil-lined baking pan.
6. Decorate the top with nuts, candied fruit, etc., if desired.
7. Chill at least 2 hours to set the chocolate completely.
8. Optionally, roll in ground Ghirardelli Bittersweet Chocolate before storing.
This recipe makes approximately 12 truffles.
